sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è risk-off a causa dell'aumento delle tensioni geopolitiche tra Iran e Israele, con un impatto negativo sui mercati azionari e un aumento della domanda di asset rifugio. Il VIX è sceso leggermente a 23.87, ma rimane sopra la soglia di 20, indicando un mercato ancora difensivo. Il prezzo del petrolio è salito del 7.78% a $109.03, mentre l'oro è sceso del 2.75% a $4651.50. Il Bitcoin è stabile intorno ai $66,920.
sentiment.reason_en: Market sentiment is risk-off due to escalating geopolitical tensions between Iran and Israel, negatively impacting equity markets and increasing demand for safe-haven assets. The VIX decreased slightly to 23.87 but remains above 20, indicating a defensive market. Oil prices surged 7.78% to $109.03, while gold declined 2.75% to $4651.50. Bitcoin is stable around $66,920.

market_impact_summary.it: I mercati azionari sono chiusi per il weekend, ma i movimenti delle crypto e delle materie prime sono significativi. Il prezzo del petrolio è aumentato del 7.78% a $109.03, mentre l'oro è sceso del 2.75% a $4651.50. Il Bitcoin è stabile intorno ai $66,920. Il VIX è sceso leggermente a 23.87.
market_impact_summary.en: Equity markets are closed for the weekend, but movements in crypto and commodities are notable. Oil prices surged 7.78% to $109.03, while gold declined 2.75% to $4651.50. Bitcoin is stable around $66,920. The VIX decreased slightly to 23.87.

audio_script_it:
Buongiorno e benvenuti all'aggiornamento finanziario di oggi, il vostro Morning Briefing quotidiano.

Mentre le borse mondiali osservano la consueta pausa festiva per il fine settimana, i riflettori restano accesi sul comparto digitale, che opera 24 ore su 24. La chiusura dei mercati asiatici ieri ha visto il Nikkei guadagnare l'1,26% a 53,123 punti e lo Shanghai Composite perdere l'1% a 3880,10 punti.

Il sentiment di risk-off caratterizza la chiusura precedente dei mercati occidentali. L'S&P 500 ha chiuso con un guadagno marginale dello 0,11% a 6582,69 punti, mentre il VIX, indice della paura, scende del 2,73% a 23,87 punti. Il Dollar Index (DXY) guadagna lo 0,16% a 100,19 punti, e il tasso del decennale USA scende di 0,14% al 4,31%.

Sul fronte geopolitico, permane alta tensione per le possibili implicazioni dell'evolversi della situazione in Medio Oriente. Gli investitori continuano a monitorare da vicino l'impatto potenziale sui prezzi dell'energia e sulla supply chain globale.

Dai dati macroeconomici, segnaliamo il recente tasso BCE (Refi/Mutui) al 2,15%. In agenda, prossime uscite importanti come il PIL QoQ USA e i dati sull'inflazione.

Passando al comparto digitale, Bitcoin mostra una sostanziale stabilità intorno ai 66,920 dollari con una variazione giornaliera del -0,02%. Tra le altre criptovalute, Ethereum scambia a 2,054 dollari con un calo del 0,45%, mentre Solana guadagna l'1,21% a 80,27 dollari. Il Fear & Greed Index indica ancora Extreme Fear a 11 punti.

In attesa della ripresa degli scambi, l'attenzione degli investitori rimarrà focalizzata sugli sviluppi geopolitici e sui prossimi dati economici. Un'analisi più approfondita sui movimenti delle criptovalute e ulteriori dettagli sui mercati tradizionali saranno oggetto di attenzione nei prossimi aggiornamenti.

Passiamo ora al comparto degli asset digitali, con una analisi approfondita delle criptovalute.

Bitcoin è sceso leggermente, rimanendo comunque sopra i $66,000. Gli ETF su Bitcoin hanno registrato un afflusso giornaliero di $9.0M. Tecnicamente, il prezzo di Bitcoin sembra stabilizzarsi dopo le recenti oscillazioni.

Ethereum è sceso dello 0.45% a $2,054, mentre Solana e Binance Coin hanno mostrato una maggiore resilienza, con guadagni dell'1.21% e dello 0.79% rispettivamente. Solana è salito a $80.27 e Binance Coin a $591.42.

L'indice Fear & Greed è a 11, in territorio di Estrema Paura. Questo livello di paura potrebbe indicare una possibile inversione del trend nel breve termine. Il contesto macroeconomico, con tassi di interesse stabili e dati sull'inflazione in linea con le aspettative, non sembra avere un impatto significativo sulle criptovalute in questo momento.

In generale, il mercato delle criptovalute sembra essere in una fase di consolidamento, con Bitcoin che cerca di stabilizzarsi sopra i $66,000 e le altcoin che mostrano segnali contrastanti. Gli investitori rimangono prudenti, come indicato dall'indice Fear & Greed in territorio di Estrema Paura.

Grazie per l'attenzione e a domani
audio_script_en:
Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.

Global sentiment is risk-off today, with investors cautious amid rising tensions in the Middle East. Key data points include a decline in the S&P 500 to 6582.69 and a significant increase in the price of Brent crude to $109.03.

Traditional markets are closed for the weekend. In the last close, equities showed mixed results: the S&P 500 was up 0.11% to 6582.69, while the STOXX 600 was down 0.20% to 59.24. The Dollar Index rose 0.16% to 100.19. The Euro to US Dollar exchange rate decreased by 0.17% to 1.15. In the bond market, the US 10-year yield decreased by 0.14% to 4.31%, and the TLT Bond USA 20Y increased by 0.61% to 86.79. Commodities saw significant movements, with Brent crude up 7.78% to $109.03 and Gold down 2.75% to $4651.50 per ounce.

Geopolitical tensions, particularly in the Middle East, are influencing market sentiment. The ongoing conflict has led to a surge in oil prices and a flight to safe-haven assets. The recent US Non-Farm Payrolls data showed an increase of 178K, while the unemployment rate was 4.3%. Inflation concerns remain, with the PCE Core YoY at 3.1%. The Federal Reserve's current interest rate is between 3.50% and 3.75%. In the Eurozone, the GDP growth rate was 0.2% Quarter over Quarter, and the ECB recently adjusted its deposit rate to 2.00%.

While traditional markets observe their holiday break, the spotlight remains on digital assets, which are open 24/7. Let's take a look at the current state of the crypto market.

Moving on to the digital assets space...

Let's dive deeper into Bitcoin's current technical analysis. The recent BTC ETF daily net inflow of $9.0M suggests a positive sentiment among investors. The price of BTC is currently at $66,930, up 0.31% from the previous day. From a technical standpoint, we can see that the inflows are supporting the price, but we need to monitor the resistance levels above $70K.

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and take a look at some of the major altcoins. Ethereum is currently trading at $2,054, down 0.45% from the previous day. Solana and Binance Coin are showing more positive movement, with SOL up 1.21% at $80.27 and BNB up 0.79% at $591.42. The performance of these altcoins indicates a mixed sentiment in the market.

In terms of market sentiment and fear/greed index, we are currently at 11, which indicates extreme fear. This could be a sign that investors are cautious, but it also presents a buying opportunity. Historically, extreme fear has been a good indicator for a potential market bounce. The correlation with traditional markets remains high, and investors are closely watching the macroeconomic indicators such as the US CPI and PCE Core YoY. As we approach the next economic data releases, it's essential to keep an eye on how these factors influence the crypto market.

Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another market update. Until then, stay informed and stay ahead. A quick look at what's on the radar for tomorrow and we'll wrap up here. Have a great day and we'll catch you soon.

articles.title: Fund managers snap up bonds on growth threat from Iran war

articles.title_en: Fund managers snap up bonds on growth threat from Iran war

articles.title: The realities of economic security
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics presents a high-level analytical discussion of the realities of economic security, examining real-world trade-offs and vulnerabilities in today's geopolitically fractured world. Anjali Bhatt, host of PIIE Insider Live, moderates a candid discussion with PIIE experts: Warwick McKibbin, who presents global macroeconomic scenarios of the impact of the Middle East conflict; Cullen Hendrix, who discusses vulnerabilities of the global economy to the disrupted supply of commodities including food, fuel, and industrial inputs; and Jacob Funk…

articles.title: When Fools Go to War
articles.snippet: One need only look past the moral and strategic differences between Iran and Ukraine to see that both are facing similar situations. Both have been attacked by larger powers whose institutional decline has produced regimes that failed to anticipate what they were setting into motion.

articles.title: The future economic architecture of the eurozone
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics is pleased to host a high-level panel discussion on the future economic architecture of the eurozone, featuring Spanish first vice president and finance minister Carlos Cuerpo Caballero and ECB Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel, alongside leading experts Olivier Blanchard of PIIE and Ángel Ubide of Citadel. Following opening remarks by Cuerpo Caballero, the conversation explores how much monetary policy transmission and financial stability in the eurozone depend on deeper integration, the opportunities and challenges posed by rising long…

articles.title: Central banking for open economies in a changed world
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics holds a high-level discussion with central bank governors addressing some of the most pressing issues in open-economy monetary policy. The conversation focuses on the implications of profound changes in the international financial and geopolitical environment. The panel, moderated by Patrick Honohan, PIIE nonresident senior fellow and former governor of the Bank of Ireland, includes central bank governors Anna Breman (Reserve Bank of New Zealand), José Luis Escrivá (Bank of Spain), Chang Yong Rhee (Bank of Korea), and Martin Schlegel (Swiss…

articles.title: The Limits of Trump’s Regime Capture Strategy from Venezuela to Cuba
articles.snippet: Since the beginning of the year, President Donald Trump has enthusiastically reinstated regime change as a key tool of U.S. statecraft, despite previously campaigning against the military excesses of former presidents. This drastic foreign policy reversal owes much to his administration’s dramatic capture of former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro in a successful raid and arrest on Jan. 3. Clearly encouraged by the apparent ease with which this act of leadership decapitation converted a one-time regional adversary into something Trump can present as a vassal state virtually overnight, the administration has sought to replicate this success in other revisionist states, most The post The Limits of Trump’s Regime Capture Strategy from Venezuela to Cuba appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: How the War with Iran Ends
articles.snippet: Three weeks into the joint American-Israeli military operation against Iran, a pressing question occupies Washington: What will ultimately follow these strikes? The attacks themselves are already degrading Tehran’s military capacity, but the more crucial focus is the aftermath — specifically, whether the pressures now weighing on the theocracy point to a negotiated settlement, prolonged attrition, or the collapse of the Islamic Republic from within.This month, I turn 40. I was born in Iraq, in the middle of my country’s eight-year war with Iran — another war that also involved this same regime. Forty years later, I find myself watching what The post How the War with Iran Ends appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: How the Iran War Became NATO’s Biggest Crisis
articles.snippet: President Donald Trump told Reuters on Wednesday that he is considering withdrawing the United States from the 76-year-old alliance after European allies declined to send warships to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to global shipping. "Wouldn't you if you were me?" he asked. Earlier this week, in an interview with Britain's The Telegraph, he called NATO a "paper tiger" and said Russian President Vladimir Putin shared the assessment. The spat has a straightforward trigger. The Strait of Hormuz, the narrow chokepoint between Iran and Oman through which…

articles.title: Oil Rally Accelerates as Traders Price in Real Supply Disruption
articles.snippet: May WTI crude oil surged sharply in the holiday-shortened week ending April 3, settling at $111.54, up $11.90 or +11.94%. The move was driven by a combination of geopolitical escalation, supply disruption risks, and growing concerns about global oil infrastructure security. Traders were forced to rapidly reprice risk, with volatility spiking across the energy complex as headlines intensified into the close. War Risk and Supply Shock Drive Breakout The dominant catalyst behind this week’s rally was the rapid escalation in tensions between…

articles.title: First Western European Vessel Transits Hormuz Since War Began
articles.snippet: A container ship owned by French shipping giant CMA CGM, has just transited the Strait of Hormuz in what appears to be the first vessel linked to Western Europe to have successfully braved the critical chokepoint since the war in the Middle East began. The Malta-flagged CMA CGM Kribi, which openly broadcasts its French ownership, transited the Strait of Hormuz between Thursday afternoon and Friday morning, according to vessel-tracking data monitored by Bloomberg. Data on MarineTraffic showed that CMA CGM Kribi was in the Oman Gulf as of 12 p.m.…

articles.title: Trump Loses Grip as Oil Surge Signals Deeper Crisis
articles.snippet: For the first time in weeks, Donald Trump failed to cool oil markets, as escalating tensions with Iran crushed ceasefire hopes and reignited bullish momentum. Friday, April 03, 2026 For the first time in many weeks, US President Trump couldn’t contain a late-week price surge as escalating tensions between Washington and Tehran dulled market bears’ expectations of a ceasefire anytime soon. WTI soared this week and is on par with ICE Brent (even if they trade different months), physical benchmarks above $140 per barrel bring back memories…

articles.title: UAE's Biggest Gas Plant Forced Offline for Second Time Since War Began
articles.snippet: Operations at the Habshan gas facilities, the biggest gas processing site in the United Arab Emirates, were suspended early on Friday following a fire that erupted after an attack, Abu Dhabi Media Office said. The Habshan onshore facilities are part of one of the world's largest gas processing plants, which is operated by Abu Dhabi's national oil company ADNOC. The five plants of the vast Habshan Complex have 14 processing trains and 6.1 bscfd capacity, according to the company. This is the second time operations at Habshan have been suspended…

articles.title: Is Bitcoin mispricing a prolonged Iran war? Ex-hedge fund manager weighs in
articles.snippet: In a recent Cointelegraph interview, macro investor James Lavish explains why markets are pricing in a quick end to the Iran war — and what could happen if that assumption is wrong.
articles.title_it: Is Bitcoin mispricing a prolonged Iran war? Ex-hedge fund manager weighs in

articles.title: Circle to launch cirBTC wrapped Bitcoin, challenging BitGo and Coinbase
articles.snippet: Circle, known for issuing stablecoins including USDC and EURC, is expanding into the Bitcoin space, targeting institutional users.

articles.title: Rocky US economy, private credit stress, war impact Bitcoin’s odds for $75K rally
articles.snippet: Are Bitcoin’s odds for a rally to $75,000 diminished by a weakening US economy, the war in Iran and multiple institutional BTC holders selling in the open market?
articles.title_it: Rocky US economy, private credit stress, war impact Bitcoin’s odds for $75K rally
articles.title_en: Rocky US economy, private credit stress, war impact Bitcoin’s odds for $75K rally

articles.title: Riot Platforms Sells 3,778 Bitcoin in Q1 as Miner Strategy Shifts Toward AI Infrastructure
articles.snippet: Bitcoin Magazine Riot Platforms Sells 3,778 Bitcoin in Q1 as Miner Strategy Shifts Toward AI Infrastructure Riot Platforms offloaded 3,778 BTC in Q1 — more than 2.5x its production — as it pivots from mining to AI infrastructure. This post Riot Platforms Sells 3,778 Bitcoin in Q1 as Miner Strategy Shifts Toward AI Infrastructure first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .
